text:
----Security_Multipart(Thu_May_27_10_46_27_2010_043)--
Content-Type: Text/Plain; charset=us-ascii
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit

Stephen Hurd <shurd@sasktel.net> wrote
  in <201005270006.o4R06CHx068904@www.freebsd.org>:

sh> >Description:
sh> When route6d is running, it will delete the site-local routes after
sh> about five minutes.
sh> >How-To-Repeat:
sh> Set up a site-local IPv6 address as well as a public one on an
sh> interface and enable routing etc (My config snippit follows) and wait
sh> five minutes.
sh>
sh> The route gets deleted and not logged to /var/log/route.log, but route
sh> monitor output shows the PID (812) of route6d deleting the route (log
sh> snippit follows).

 Can you try the -D option (foreground + debug) and send me a log when
 the route is deleted if possible?

-- Hiroki
